common.tagline: Smart, personalized communication for children with autism

error.title: Something went wrong
error.description: Please try again. If the problem continues, contact support.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has moved.

footer.originNote: Born from a senior research project at Jeddah International College.

trustStrip.lead: Born from a senior research project at

hero.title: Help your child be heard.
hero.subtitle: BlueCare turns symbols, voice, and gestures into the words your child wants to say — in Arabic and English, on whatever device you already own. Free, open, and built to be in every family's pocket.
hero.freeCaption: Free for families, therapists, and educators. No credit card.
hero.screenshotAlt: BlueCare communication board with symbol tiles in Arabic and English

features.title: Communication that meets your child where they are.
features.subtitle: Three input modes, one calm experience. Choose what works in the moment — your child can mix them freely.

symbols.title: Symbols
symbols.body: A bilingual pictogram board your child can tap. Vocabulary grows with them — the right tiles surface when they're most likely to be needed.

voice.title: Voice
voice.body: Hold-to-speak in Arabic or English. We turn approximations into clear sentences your child can play back, never recording more than the moment.

gestures.title: Gestures
gestures.body: An optional camera mode recognizes pinch-to-select and swipe-to-navigate — entirely on the device. Video never leaves your home.

personalization.title: Learns your child's words. Quietly.
personalization.subtitle: BlueCare watches usage patterns — never content — to suggest the next vocabulary tile, the right time of day for new words, and a difficulty curve your child can actually climb.
personalization.bullet1: Caregiver review on every suggestion before it changes the board.
personalization.bullet2: Per-child monthly AI cost cap, enforced in code.
personalization.bullet3: No third-party tracking on your child's input — ever.

family.title: Families
family.body: A calm, predictable companion at home. Add custom symbols, record your own voice, and watch progress without pressure.

therapists.title: Therapists
therapists.body: Multi-client caseloads with shared dashboards, vocabulary curation, and exportable progress reports — built around how your sessions actually flow.

schools.title: Schools
schools.body: Classroom-ready, bilingual, and accessible to non-verbal learners. District-friendly deployment without analytics on student input.

closingCta.title: Every child has something to say.
closingCta.subtitle: BlueCare is free and open to families, therapists, and schools. Create an account in under a minute.

howItWorks.title: How BlueCare works
howItWorks.subtitle: Four steps from setup to your child's first sentence.

setup.title: 1. Set up your child's profile
setup.body: Tell us their name, language, sensory preferences, and starting vocabulary level. Every choice has a calm default if you're not sure.

communicate.title: 2. Open the board
communicate.body: Your child taps symbols, holds to speak, or uses optional camera gestures. BlueCare reads the sentence aloud in the voice you chose.

learn.title: 3. The board learns
learn.body: Vocabulary suggestions are reviewed by you before they appear. The grid reorders so the words your child actually uses stay close at hand.

share.title: 4. Share with the team
share.body: Therapists you invite see progress and session replays. You stay in control — invite codes are yours to revoke at any time.

items.q: Does my child need to be verbal?
items.a: No. BlueCare is built for non-verbal and minimally verbal children. Symbols and gestures work without any speech at all.
Arabic and English at launch, with full right-to-left layout, Arabic phonetic helpers, and culturally appropriate symbols.

items.q: Do I need a special device?
items.a: Any modern tablet, phone, or laptop works. The child board is tablet-first; the caregiver dashboard works on phones too.

items.q: Is video sent to a server when I use gestures?
items.a: No. Hand tracking runs entirely on the device. Camera frames never leave the browser unless you opt in.

items.q: Who can see my child's data?
items.a: Only you, by default. Therapists can be invited and revoked at any time. We never sell data and we never use a child's input to train a model.
You can export everything (sessions, vocabulary, progress) and delete your account from the dashboard. We honor deletion requests within 30 days.

hero.title: A calm tool for the family.
hero.subtitle: BlueCare is designed for the long evenings and the small breakthroughs. No surprises, no pressure, no flashy celebrations.

items.title: Custom vocabulary
items.body: Add your child's favorite snacks, a sibling's name, the route to grandma's house. Bilingual labels, your own recorded voice, optional phonetic help.

items.title: Quiet mode
items.body: One toggle removes every animation and sound. For meltdown days, dental visits, or quiet rooms.

items.title: Progress without pressure
items.body: Streaks cap at five stars a day on purpose. We celebrate calmly. We never use leaderboards.

items.title: Session replays
items.body: Watch what your child said today, in the order they said it. Add a note, share with a therapist, or save it for the file.

items.title: Parental PIN
items.body: Settings, voice clips, and the dashboard sit behind a PIN your child won't accidentally tap into.

items.title: Your data is yours
items.body: Export everything as PDF or JSON. Delete your account in two clicks. We honor deletion requests within 30 days, end-to-end.

hero.title: Built around how you actually run sessions.
hero.subtitle: Multi-client caseloads, exportable reports, and a vocabulary-curation tool that respects clinical judgment over algorithmic guesses.

items.title: Caseload at a glance
items.body: All your invited clients on one screen. Today's session, streak, top symbols, and what changed since you last logged in.

items.title: Vocabulary curation
items.body: Approve or reject every AI suggestion. Add your own symbols. Pin clinical-priority tiles. The board is the team's decision, not the model's.

items.title: Session replay with notes
items.body: Step through inputs and outputs in sequence. Add notes attached to specific moments. Export the report as PDF for the file.

items.title: Bilingual by design
items.body: Switch a client's board between Arabic and English with one toggle. Phonetic helpers, culturally appropriate symbols, full RTL layout.

items.title: Quiet by default
items.body: No popups, no animations, no leaderboards. The tool stays out of the way during a session.

items.title: Privacy posture you can defend
items.body: No third-party analytics on a child's input. Encrypted at rest and in transit. Audit log on every clinically relevant action.

hero.title: From a research project to a real tool.
hero.subtitle: BlueCare started as a senior project in the Computer Science department at Jeddah International College. Four students saw a gap — culturally Arabic, AI-personalized AAC tools simply did not exist — and decided to fill it.

story.title: The origin story
story.p1: Existing AAC tools are rigid. They're monolingual. They were never designed for Arabic-speaking families, and they don't learn from how a specific child actually communicates. PECS books are static. Static SGDs feel clinical. The market gap was visible from the first interview the team did with families and speech therapists in Jeddah.
story.p2: The senior project posed a simple question: can a modern, multimodal, bilingual AAC platform actually adapt to a child over time without becoming creepy or expensive? The answer in the report was yes. The engineering build is now turning that answer into shipping software.
story.p3: Today BlueCare is being prepared for a real market launch. The same four authors are still on it. The supervisor — Dr. Hasanin Barhamtoshy — still steers the academic shape of it. The bar is now consumer healthtech, not student project.

items.title: Calm beats clever
items.body: Predictable, low-sensory-load interfaces. No leaderboards, no time pressure, no flashy celebrations.

items.title: Arabic is a first-class citizen
items.body: Right-to-left from the first pixel. Phonetic helpers. Culturally appropriate symbols, not English imports with translated labels.

items.title: Children's data is sacred
items.body: No third-party analytics on child input. No model trained on a child's voice without explicit consent. Audit log on every privileged action.

items.title: Caregivers stay in control
items.body: AI suggestions are caregiver-reviewable by default. Every personalization decision the system makes is visible and reversible.

hero.title: Four authors, one supervisor, and a long list of advisors.
hero.subtitle: BlueCare's authors are the four senior-project students who scoped, designed, and proposed the system. They are now leading it through to launch.

joinUs.title: Join us
joinUs.body: We're hiring no one yet — the team is small on purpose. If you're a speech therapist, a parent of a child with autism, or an Arabic-speaking AAC researcher, we'd love to hear from you.

hero.title: We protect children's data the way we'd protect our own.
hero.subtitle: BlueCare is engineered to a HIPAA-aware standard with privacy posture aligned to Saudi PDPL and GDPR-style rights. Below is what that actually means in practice.

items.title: Encrypted at rest
items.body: All persisted data — children's profiles, session events, vocabulary, audio clips, audit logs — is encrypted at rest in our managed Postgres.

items.title: Encrypted in transit
items.body: TLS 1.3 everywhere. HSTS preloaded. Strict referrer policy and a content-security-aware permissions policy on every response.

items.title: Row-level security on every table
items.body: A caregiver can only see their own children. A therapist can only see clients they've been explicitly invited to. Admins can never read child input content. The database itself enforces it, not just the application.

items.title: Least-privilege roles
items.body: Service-role access is gated to specific server-only paths. Application code uses the user's JWT so RLS triggers on every query.

items.title: Audit log on every privileged action
items.body: Sign-in, profile updates, child creation, symbol moderation, data exports, and consent changes all write a row to an append-only audit log. Admins read; nobody updates or deletes.

items.title: Per-child AI cost cap
items.body: Every paid AI call (speech transcription, suggestions, voice synthesis) passes through a single guard that enforces a monthly per-child budget — preventing runaway cost or runaway use.

items.title: No third-party tracking on child surfaces
items.body: The child board never includes third-party analytics. Caregiver dashboards include only product analytics that exclude any child input content.

items.title: On-device gesture recognition
items.body: Hand tracking runs in the browser via MediaPipe. Camera frames never leave the device unless a caregiver opts in for a specific session.

items.title: Export
items.body: One click downloads everything we have on your account — profiles, sessions, vocabulary, audit log entries — as a portable archive.

items.title: Delete
items.body: One click deletes your account. We tombstone immediately and purge within 30 days end-to-end, including backups within their retention window.

items.title: Revoke consent
items.body: Each consent scope (data processing, voice recording, webcam, AI personalization) can be revoked independently at any time.

items.title: Inspect
items.body: The audit log is visible to admins. We will share the entries that pertain to your account on request.

disclosure.title: Vulnerability disclosure
disclosure.body: If you find a security issue, please email security@bluecare.app. We commit to acknowledging within 48 hours and fixing within reasonable timelines based on severity. We don't run a paid bounty yet — but we publicly thank reporters with permission.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.intro: BlueCare is engineered to handle children's data with care. This page describes what we collect, why, where it lives, and what choices you have. It reflects how the product actually works — not generic templates.

whoWeAre.title: Who we are
whoWeAre.body: BlueCare is operated by the BlueCare team, originating from a senior project at Jeddah International College, Computer Science department. For data-protection inquiries, email privacy@bluecare.app.

whatWeCollect.title: What we collect
whatWeCollect.body: Account-level data: caregiver email, name, locale, theme preference. Child-profile data (only data the caregiver enters): preferred name, age, language, sensory profile, vocabulary level, voice selection. Usage events: which symbols a child selected, when, and via which input modality (symbol/voice/gesture). We do NOT store raw transcripts of a child's speech. We do NOT keep video frames from gesture mode beyond the moment they are processed.

whyWeCollect.title: Why we collect it
whyWeCollect.body: (1) To run the product — without a profile we cannot show a board. (2) To personalize vocabulary suggestions, which a caregiver reviews before they take effect. (3) To build progress views for caregivers and therapists. We do NOT collect for advertising. We do NOT sell data. We do NOT train models on child input.

lawfulBasis.title: Lawful basis
lawfulBasis.body: For caregivers in Saudi Arabia, we process under PDPL Article 5 with explicit caregiver consent. For users in the EEA / UK, we process under GDPR Articles 6(1)(a) (consent) and 6(1)(b) (contract). Each consent scope (data processing, voice recording, webcam processing, AI personalization, analytics dashboard) can be granted or revoked independently.

thirdParties.title: Third parties
thirdParties.body: We use Supabase (managed Postgres + Auth + Storage) for application data, hosted in Sydney. Voice synthesis routes through two TTS providers: ElevenLabs Multilingual v2 (Charlotte voice) for Arabic, and OpenAI tts-1-hd (Nova voice) for English; both transparently fall back to each other on outage. Speech recognition uses OpenAI Whisper. Optional vocabulary personalization uses Anthropic Claude Sonnet ONLY when a caregiver explicitly enables it. Every cloud TTS call is metered against a per-child 0/month cost cap. Audio data sent to the speech provider is never persisted on our side; it lives in memory only for the duration of the request.

retention.title: How long we keep data
retention.body: Active accounts: as long as you keep them. Audit log entries: 24 months. Anonymized aggregate metrics: indefinitely (no individual identifiable). On account deletion: tombstoned immediately, purged within 30 days end-to-end, including from backups within their retention window.

yourRights.title: Your rights
yourRights.body: Access, rectification, erasure, restriction, portability, and objection — all available from the dashboard or by emailing privacy@bluecare.app. We respond within 30 days. You can also lodge a complaint with the Saudi Data & AI Authority or your local supervisory authority.

children.title: About children's data
children.body: Children do not authenticate directly. A child's profile is owned by a caregiver. Caregivers attest to consent on behalf of the child for each scope. We minimize the data we collect about children, never expose it to third-party analytics, and treat it as the most sensitive class of data in the system.

changes.title: Changes
changes.body: We will update this page whenever the data flows or providers change, and we will email caregivers about any material change at least 30 days before it takes effect.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.intro: These terms describe the agreement between you and BlueCare when you use the service. They are written to be readable, not aggressive.

service.title: The service
service.body: BlueCare is an AAC web platform for children with autism. It provides a child-facing communication board, a caregiver/therapist dashboard, and an admin surface. The exact feature set evolves; significant removals are announced at least 60 days in advance.

accounts.title: Accounts
accounts.body: Caregivers and therapists create accounts. Children do not. You are responsible for keeping your credentials secure and for any actions taken under your account.

acceptableUse.title: Acceptable use
acceptableUse.body: Don't reverse-engineer, scrape, or interfere with the service. Don't use it to send unlawful content. Don't impersonate another person. Custom symbol uploads are subject to admin moderation; we may reject content that conflicts with our content policy.

ip.title: Intellectual property
ip.body: BlueCare, the brand mark, and the codebase are owned by the BlueCare team. ARASAAC pictograms are licensed under CC BY-NC-SA, attributed in the in-app credits. Custom symbols you upload remain yours; you grant us a limited license to display them to your account.

fees.title: Fees
fees.body: BlueCare is free for everyone — families, therapists, and educators alike. We do not charge subscription fees, seat fees, or per-child fees. Operational AI costs are governed by a per-child monthly cap inside the application; the system gracefully degrades (cached responses, fewer suggestions) when a cap is reached, never paywalled. If we ever introduce paid features, they will be additive and announced at least 60 days in advance.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: You can delete your account at any time from the dashboard. We may suspend accounts that violate acceptable use, with notice where the situation allows.

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.body: BlueCare is a communication tool, not a medical device. It does not diagnose, treat, or cure any condition. We make no warranty that the service is fit for a particular clinical purpose. For clinical decisions, consult your therapist.

law.title: Governing law
law.body: These terms are governed by the laws of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. Disputes go to the competent courts of Jeddah.

accessibility.title: Accessibility statement
accessibility.intro: BlueCare is built for non-verbal and minimally verbal children with autism. Accessibility is not a checklist for us — it is the product. This page describes the actual conformance state of every shipped surface as of the date above, with named known issues.

conformance.title: Conformance state per surface
conformance.summary: Marketing routes target WCAG 2.2 AA. The child communication board targets WCAG 2.2 AAA on color contrast, focus, and motion. Every surface is automatically tested with axe-core in CI plus Lighthouse on every PR; a manual screen-reader pass (VoiceOver macOS + NVDA Windows) is required before launch and is currently scheduled in Module 9 hardening.
tested: Marketing site (/, /signup, /login, /accessibility, /privacy, /terms, /help, /help/*) — WCAG 2.2 AA, axe-core 0 serious/critical, manual SR pass pending Module 9.

Communication board (/[locale]/board) — WCAG 2.2 AAA on color contrast, focus rings, and motion; full keyboard navigation (Tab + arrows + Space + Enter); 44×44px minimum touch targets; prefers-reduced-motion respected.
Caregiver dashboard (/[locale]/dashboard and sub-routes) — WCAG 2.2 AA, axe-core clean. Session replay and PDF reports tested for keyboard + screen-reader. Manual SR pass pending Module 9.
Settings (/[locale]/settings/*) — WCAG 2.2 AA, every form field labeled, parental-PIN entry has live aria-status updates.
Admin (/[locale]/admin and sub-routes) — WCAG 2.2 AA; j/k keyboard nav announced via aria-keyshortcuts.
Therapist surface (/[locale]/therapist) — WCAG 2.2 AA, caseload grid is keyboard-traversable.
High-contrast theme — meets WCAG AAA contrast (7:1+) on every text-on-background pair.
Right-to-left layout parity — every component, icon, and animation respects writing direction; tested on every page in /ar.

knownIssues.title: Known issues
knownIssues.body: Two named items as of this revision. (1) Manual VoiceOver + NVDA screen-reader pass on the new Module 6.1 session replay, /admin, and /therapist surfaces is scheduled for Module 9 production hardening but not yet recorded. axe-core CI passes on these surfaces, but human SR review may surface improvements. (2) Symbol audit run on 2026-05-12 flagged 33 image-label mismatches, of which the vast majority are AAC iconography conventions (pointing-finger for you, arrows for grow) that Claude vision judged too literally — a refined audit prompt landing in Module 9 should reduce real mismatches to ≤ 5. We do not auto-relabel based on the current flagged set; the underlying symbol labels are correct.

attributions.title: Attributions
attributions.arasaac: Pictogram artwork on the BlueCare communication board is © ARASAAC, Government of Aragón (Spain), and is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-ShareAlike 4.0 International (CC BY-NC-SA 4.0). Source: arasaac.org.

feedback.title: Feedback
feedback.body: If you encounter a barrier, please tell us. We treat accessibility regressions as bugs of the highest severity. Email accessibility@bluecare.app or use the contact form.

contact.title: Contact us
contact.subtitle: We answer email. We don't run a chat widget. If you have a security issue, please use the dedicated address.

testimonials: BlueCare gives my son his voice back.
The bilingual board is the first AAC tool that meets our family where we are.
I can finally see what works for my client between sessions — without paperwork.

signup.title: Create your BlueCare account.
signup.subtitle: Free for everyone. No credit card. One form for families, therapists, and school staff.
signup.roleHelper: Choose one. We'll tailor the next steps to fit.

family.label: Parent or family
family.description: I want to help my child communicate.

therapist.label: Therapist or specialist
therapist.description: I work with children with ASD.

school.label: School staff
school.description: I'm an educator, SLP, or school admin.

onboarding.title: You're in.
onboarding.subtitle: We're putting the finishing touches on the onboarding wizard. The full 8-step flow — child profile, sensory preferences, vocabulary level, voice selection, consent scopes, parental PIN, review — ships with the next module update.
onboarding.previewBoard: See what the child board will look like

errors.csrf: Your session needs a quick refresh. Please tap Continue again to retry.
errors.signedOut: You've been signed out. Please sign in again to keep going.
errors.offline: We couldn't reach our servers. Check your connection and tap Continue again.
errors.generic: Something went wrong on our end. Please tap Continue again — your progress is safe.

welcome.title: Welcome to BlueCare.
welcome.body: We'll set up your child's communication board in eight calm steps. You can save and come back anytime — nothing is lost.

aboutYou.title: About you
aboutYou.subtitle: Just enough so the dashboard greets you correctly.
aboutYou.fullNameError: Please enter your full name (2–80 characters).

aboutChild.title: About your child
aboutChild.subtitle: Only what we need to show the right board. You can edit anything later.
aboutChild.preferredNamePlaceholder: What they like to be called

sensory.title: Sensory preferences
sensory.subtitle: We'll calibrate motion, sound, contrast, and touch targets to keep the experience calm.

audio.label: Audio
audio.helper: How loud should feedback sounds be?

touch.label: Touch targets
touch.helper: How large should the symbol tiles be?

vocabularyLevel.title: Starting vocabulary level
vocabularyLevel.subtitle: We'll seed the board to match. Caregivers can always promote or hide tiles later.

starter.label: Starter
starter.description: Core needs first — eat, drink, more, help, yes, no. About 30 tiles.

expanding.label: Expanding
expanding.description: Adds people, places, feelings, and a small action set. About 80 tiles.

conversational.label: Conversational
conversational.description: Sentence-building with verbs, descriptors, and time. About 200 tiles.

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.description: Full bilingual board with categories, school, and social. 500+ tiles.

voice.title: Choose a voice
voice.subtitle: Tap Listen to compare. Both voices work in English and Arabic.

charlotte.label: Warm
charlotte.description: Soft, gentle pitch. A calm reading voice.

sarah.label: Soft
sarah.description: Lower energy. Good for quieter rooms.

consent.title: Consent
consent.subtitle: Each scope is independent. You can revoke any of these later in Settings → Privacy.
consent.coreRequired: Data processing consent is required to continue.

data_processing.label: Data processing
data_processing.description: Store the profile data above so we can show your child's board on every device.

ai_personalization.label: AI personalization
ai_personalization.description: Use usage patterns (never content) to suggest the next vocabulary tile. You review every change before it lands.

voice_recording.label: Voice recording
voice_recording.description: Hold-to-speak captures short audio that's transcribed and discarded. Recordings never leave the request.

webcam_processing.label: Webcam processing
webcam_processing.description: Optional gesture mode runs entirely on-device. No video frames are uploaded.

analytics_dashboard.label: Caregiver analytics
analytics_dashboard.description: Show usage analytics on your dashboard. Excludes any child-input content.

pin.title: Set a parental PIN
pin.subtitle: A 6-digit PIN keeps Settings, Vocabulary, and account actions safe from accidental taps.

review.title: Review and finish
review.subtitle: One last look. You can edit anything later from Settings.
review.trust: Encrypted at rest. Audit-logged. You can delete or export anytime.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.subtitle: Each consent scope can be revoked independently. Revoking writes a new audit-logged record — we never mutate history.

data_processing.label: Data processing
data_processing.description: Storage of caregiver/child profile data needed to run the product.

ai_personalization.label: AI personalization
ai_personalization.description: Usage-pattern-based vocabulary suggestions, with caregiver review before they take effect.

voice_recording.label: Voice recording
voice_recording.description: Short hold-to-speak audio capture, transcribed and discarded inside the request.

webcam_processing.label: Webcam processing
webcam_processing.description: On-device gesture recognition. Camera frames never leave the device.

analytics_dashboard.label: Caregiver analytics
analytics_dashboard.description: Product analytics in your dashboard. Excludes any child-input content.

privacy.historyNote: Revoking writes a new record — your full consent history is preserved for audit.

therapists.title: Therapist invites
therapists.subtitle: Issue a single-use 12-character code. Codes expire in 7 days. You can revoke an unused code at any time.
therapists.noInvites: No invites yet — issue your first to share your child's board with a therapist.

board.title: Board
board.subtitle: Tap symbols, hold to speak, or use camera gestures to build a sentence and have BlueCare read it aloud.
board.starHint: Tap Speak to read it aloud and earn a star.
board.transcribeSlowHint: Transcription is slow today — try a shorter clip.
board.attribution: Symbols: ARASAAC · Creative Commons BY-NC-SA · arasaac.org

sentence.placeholder: Tap symbols to build a sentence.

sttReasons.no_browser_api: Voice input isn't supported in this browser. Try Chrome, Edge, or Safari.
sttReasons.permission_denied: Microphone permission was denied. Enable it in your browser settings to use voice input.
sttReasons.not_secure_context: Voice input only works on secure (https) sites.

error.title: We couldn't load the board.
error.body: Please try again. If the problem continues, check your connection or contact support.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Your child's communication board, settings, and progress live here. The full dashboard arrives with the next module — for now, the most important pieces are already one tap away.

board.title: Open the board
board.body: Tap symbols to build a sentence, then have BlueCare read it aloud. Hold-to-speak and camera gestures are available where the browser supports them.

personalization.title: Review suggestions
personalization.body: BlueCare proposes new symbols based on how your child uses the board. Approve or reject each one — the board only changes when you say so.

settings.title: Manage settings
settings.body: Privacy, consent, therapist invites, account export, and parental PIN — all in one place.

help.title: Read help
help.body: Quick answers, walkthroughs, and contact paths. The help center is being assembled.

roadmap.title: Coming up for your account
roadmap.body: We're shipping the rest of BlueCare in calm, focused passes. Here's what's next on the roadmap — every feature is free for every family.

items.board: Bilingual symbol board with hold-to-speak and on-device gestures.
items.personalization: Frequency-based vocabulary suggestions you review before they land.
items.gamification: Calm streaks and unlockable theme packs — no leaderboards.
items.sessionReplay: Session replays with therapist notes attached to specific moments.
items.therapistDashboard: A multi-child caseload view for invited therapists.
items.pdfReports: Exportable PDF progress reports for the file.

v6.title: Dashboard
v6.subtitle: How your child is communicating today, with calm, honest numbers — no leaderboards.
v6.subtitleFor: How {childName} is communicating today, with calm, honest numbers — no leaderboards.

today.heading: Today at a glance
today.description: Most recent activity across input modalities, success rate, and sentence length.
today.empty: Start your day with the first session — once a few symbols are tapped, this panel fills in.

modality.empty: No inputs in the last day.

sessions.heading: Last 10 sessions
sessions.description: Newest first. Tap counts and success rates come straight from the on-device event log.
sessions.empty: No sessions yet. Open the board to start one.

topSymbols.heading: Most-used (30 days)
topSymbols.description: What your child reaches for most often.
topSymbols.empty: Once a few sessions land, the top symbols list fills in.

sparkline.heading: 30-day trend
sparkline.description: How many symbols are on the board, day by day.
sparkline.altText: Vocabulary size over the last 30 days
sparkline.empty: Once a few days of activity log, this chart will show the growth curve.

suggestions.heading: Pending review
suggestions.description: Approve or reject — the board only changes when you say so.
suggestions.empty: Nothing waiting for review. New ideas land after the nightly recompute.

nextMilestone.progressBody: {mastered} of {active} tiles mastered at this level.
nextMilestone.readyForNext: Ready to advance to {next} — promotion lands tonight.
nextMilestone.emptyBody: Start with 5 symbols today. Tap, hold-to-speak, build a sentence — every tap counts toward the next milestone.

streak.heading: Calm consistency
streak.description: Stars cap at 5 per day on purpose — we reward calm communication, not marathons.

newCaregiver.title: Set up your child's board
newCaregiver.body: Add a child profile and pick a starting vocabulary level. The dashboard fills in once the first session lands.

themes.title: Themes
themes.subtitle: Pick a calm tile theme for your child's board. New themes unlock as your child uses BlueCare across multiple days — no time pressure, no comparisons, no leaderboards.
themes.cap: Stars are capped at 5 per day on purpose. The streak counter rewards calm, consistent communication — never marathons.

default.label: Default
default.body: BlueCare's calm bilingual palette — your starting point.

animal.label: Animal
animal.body: Friendly mammal accents on the tile borders. Same calm contrast.

nature.label: Nature
nature.body: Forest greens and sandstone neutrals. Soft, low-stimulus.

space.label: Space
space.body: Deep navy with soft star highlights. Looks great in low light.

ocean.label: Ocean
ocean.body: Cool teal palette with watery accents. The calmest of the set.

insights.title: Insights
insights.subtitle: Claude-generated weekly progress for your child — strengths, growth areas, and what to try at home.

personalization.title: Personalization
personalization.subtitle: BlueCare suggests new symbols for your child's board based on how often they communicate. Every suggestion waits for your tap before it lands on the board.
personalization.footnote: Suggestions come from frequency patterns on your child's board — never from third-party tracking. The optional AI upgrade is opt-in and never paywalled.

mode.title: How suggestions arrive
mode.body: Choose whether new vocabulary lands automatically or waits for your review. The optional AI assist is per-child and stays off until you turn it on.
mode.llmUnavailable: AI assist isn't configured on this deployment.

pending.title: Pending review ({n})
pending.emptyBody: BlueCare runs personalization overnight. As your child uses the board, new tile suggestions will appear here for your review.

pinGate.title: Confirm with your PIN
pinGate.subtitle: Enter your 6-digit parental PIN to {action}.
pinGate.notSet: No PIN is set yet. Set one from Settings → Account first.
pinGate.lockedOut: Too many wrong attempts. Try again in a few minutes.

account.title: Account
account.subtitle: Export everything we have on file, or delete your account. Both honour your GDPR/PDPL rights.

export.title: Export your data
export.body: Download a portable JSON archive of every record we hold for you — profile, child profiles, sessions, vocabulary, audit log entries.
export.reauth: Please sign in again. For security, exports require a session less than five minutes old.

delete.title: Delete your account
delete.body: This tombstones your account immediately. Hard purge — including from backups within their retention window — completes within 30 days. There is no undo after the grace window.
delete.confirmedTitle: Your account is queued for deletion.
delete.confirmedBody: We've tombstoned the account immediately and will hard-delete by {date}. You'll be signed out now.
delete.reauth: Please sign in again. For security, deletion requires a session less than five minutes old.

acceptInvite.acceptedBody: You now have access to this child's board. Open the dashboard to begin.

errors.not_found: We don't recognize this code. Ask the caregiver to issue a new one.
errors.expired: This invite has expired. Ask the caregiver to issue a fresh one — they last seven days.
errors.already_accepted: This invite has already been used.
errors.revoked: This invite was revoked. Ask the caregiver to issue a new one if you still need access.
errors.unauthorized: Please sign in first, then open the invite link again.
errors.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again, or ask the caregiver to issue a new code.
